entry.title: Tax exclusion for the deposit condition type while posting the Invoice for the PO
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi sir and SAP Experts,

I have created a new condition type for Deposit in the Purchase Order. The condition is working as expected in the PO, and the deposit amount is posting to a separate G/L account.

As per Lakshmipathi sir’s suggestion, I maintained the condition relevance as Goods/Service Item + Planned Delivery Cost. During invoice posting (MIRO), the deposit amount is coming as a separate line item and is posting successfully.

However, the issue is that tax is also being calculated on the deposit amount.
The business requirement is to calculate tax only on the main material net price, and not on the deposit condition value.

I have attached the configuration screenshots for reference.
Could you please advise if any changes are required in the condition type or pricing procedure so that the deposit condition value is excluded from tax calculation?

Also, please let me know if any general configuration changes are required. I have maintained the configuration based on my understanding, and it is working correctly in the PO, but I would appreciate confirmation or guidance if anything needs to be adjusted.

Your guidance on how to meet this requirement would be highly appreciated.

M/06:

M/08:

entry.title: Re: Which SQL tools can use associations of CDS views?
entry.content: Hello, thanks for your answer. In our organization we have a strict separation in the IT team which supports the S/4 Hana (on-premise system 2023FS2, with a target to go to a private cloud). There is a functional team taking care of configuration, process setup, interaction with the business, specifying necessary ABAP developments. And there is a development team that implements what is specified. One task of the functional team is to understand which views of the standard virtual data model can be used to report on the business processes. Are all the used dimensions and measures that we enter in the business process available in a standard view. The CDS views have associations defined which describe the data object better (e.g. for a process order the relation to operations, components; and from operations to work center; and components toproduct information,...). The functional team can use this information to learn more about how data objects are structured and if the association help to get additional information. All this information is then used by the functional team to specify the custom virtual data model, (on top of the standard data model) which describes the master data and transactional data and key figures of our company. The strict separation between functional team and development team blocks the functional team to have S_DEVELOP change/create authority. This is why we look for other tools which can help to understand the existing CDS views. The App "Custom CDS Views" has two versions F1866(Deprecated) and F1866A. You can't create or change views in both if you miss authority for S_DEVELOP. Withouzt S_DEVELOP, the decprecated app F1866 displays released CDS views, the current version F1866A displays only Custom CDS views that are created with this App. Best regards Andreas Ries

entry.title: Re: Can AI Be Effectively Used to Automate Enterprise Wor...
entry.content: AI can be effectively used in enterprise workflows when it augments humans rather than replaces them. Today, AI adds the most value in high-volume, repetitive tasks like document processing, customer support triage, forecasting, and anomaly detection. Human oversight is ensured through human-in-the-loop designs, clear escalation paths, explainable models, and audit logs. Common challenges include data quality, bias, integration with legacy systems, and employee trust. AI should be avoided in contexts requiring ethical judgment, legal accountability, or nuanced human empathy. Balancing automation, accuracy, and accountability requires strong governance, continuous monitoring, and clearly defined ownership of AI-driven decisions.

entry.title: Re: Standard algorithm 001 finding matches outside define...
entry.content:
Hi @kezelda ,

I'm afraid I can't help you completely. However, have you tried to create processing rules for your bank statement processing? Processing rules are the successor feature of the posting rules, and the development team recommends you to switch to processing rules. In processing ruels, you can define conditions,a nd the rule is only activated to process bank statement items if those conditions are fulfilled. You can also select and define various actions that the processing rule should carry out. 

That means, processing rules process yourbank statement items just the way want - if the settings are determined correctly.

For more information, have a look at Processing Rules and How to Create Processing Rules. 

Best regards,
Anita

entry.title: Can PR Overall Release enforce per-item approval?
entry.content:
We are using Purchase Requisition – Overall Release workflow in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
Now each PR item has its own Cost Center and approvers depend on that Cost Center (maintained via custom table).

Example:
Item 10 (CC A --> approvers B/C)
Item 20 (CC D --> approvers E/F)
We need Item 10 to be approved by B or C and Item 20 by E or F before the PR is released. 

Is there any supported way to achieve this with Overall Release, or is Item Release workflow the only option?

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Duc,

Sorry, I've missed your message.

Indeed the feature has been implemented as part of the standard.

We have now a MALLOC_ARENA_MAX = REQUESTED_CPU × MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO with by default, MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO is 2. For example,  MALLOC_ARENA_MAX will be 20 for pods with 10 cores.

The ratio is the recommended value but if this is required to set more, Product support engineer can manually set the MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO environment variable for the relevant services.

Thanks,

Yannick

entry.title: Re: Kyma High availability
entry.content:
Hi @akuller ,

Please find details below:

Kyma is highly available inside a region. This means that the 3 nodes gets deployed on 3 different availability zones inside a region.  https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/availability-zones-in-kyma-environment 

Rearding the components, e.g. the Kyma modules are getting updated on a regular basis. They are zero downtime updates.

The major updates (e.g. Kubernetes version upgrade) follow the BTP maintenance window schedule. https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/systems-installations/cac/maintenance-windows.html?anchorId=section_1497935568

Ensuring that your applications are also highly available, you can set up multiple replicas across different availability zones. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/resilient-applications-in-kyma-environment 

BR

Gaurav

entry.title: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hello, and thanks for spending some time reading this blog post! 

Today, I want to address Compatibility Package (CP) use rights issue and how to check them in your system going forward. To know more about compatibility packs, compatibility scope matrix and way forward details please refer note 2269324.

As you might be aware, the compatibility use rights for On-Prem S/4HANA customers were extended till May 31st, 2026. After this period, customers who do not operate under a cloud subscription are not allowed to use CP functionalities anymore.

Now, what changes are expected with this new extension?

There is going to be one source of information for customers to check CP usage in their systems and it is your Simplification Item check! To know more details about this, please refer to note 2399707.

We’ve modernized our different compatibility pack usage reports and combined them in the simplification item check (Please implement the latest version of note 2399707 before proceeding further!)! Run the report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK, select the radio button “New relevance check for Compatibility Packages” and execute. Here, please note that report runs for the current SP of the system in which you would want to check the CP usage, the current SP version is selected by default once you choose radio button to check CP usage (Always make sure to run it in the system where you would want to check the CP usage, it is client dependent!).

 

 

 

Once the report is executed, you could see the list of CP items used in your system. If you would like to know their usage, then please go to application logs after the execution of report (Please check the log only after you have executed the report). Here, you could see the usage data item wise in your list. It will give you item usage data in the last 3 months (3M) and last 1 month (1M). It will also show the item as irrelevant if in the last 12 months usage was 0 (object was used 0 times in period of 12 months).

 

 

 

Once you have the data from execution of report, you could see the alternatives for those items from your way forward excel maintained in note 2269324. SAP recommends you move to alternate solutions before the expiry of your use rights!  Please reach out to your local SAP account representative to discuss your options.

For Customers operating their S/4HANA system under cloud subscription there is no change in usage rights, it is extended till end of December 2030 as communicated before!

For Customers who are not currently under the maintenance contract with SAP, Please reach out to SAP!

#Compatibility Packs                #Compatibility Usage                #Compatibility Use Rights              SAP S/4HANA         #SAP S/4HANA On-Prem

entry.title: VAT Codes clarification BE & ES
entry.content:
Hi, 

We'r implementing RITA in one of our company codes running S4 public cloud, a company situated in Netherlands with plant abroad in Belgium and Spain. We've activated RITA and set to status 5 in all of our systems, so we'r now ready to preform the configuration activity Defining Tax Accounts to add the dimension tax country. We've therefor with a help of a tax expert defined which tax-codes should be added into the the configuration, since we're expecting small businesses in the tax countries we'd like to add only a few. However, the tax expert has some questions I'll need some support with related to tax codes in BE and ES, please find the questions below: 

  1. BE: Which tax code should be used for the domestic reverse charge. Sales of goods and services in Belgium by a non-established entity to an established taxable person in Belgium is subject to a domestic (local) reverse charge. This would likely be applicable if company code imports products and sells the within Belgium (B2B)?
  2. BE: What does tax code 99 (Sale BE Exempt VAT-Unit) refer to. Is that meant to be used in case we would have local exempt sales?  
  3. ES: What should tax code “AF” be used to (“Non subject due localization rules – Services”)?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: Tax exclusion for the deposit condition type while po...
entry.content:
• If MWST is set to calculate tax from PBXX (material price) up to a line number that includes your deposit condition, you need to change the “To” so it stops before the deposit condition line.
  • Example:
    • PBXX = line 100
    • Deposit = line 200
    • MWST currently “From 100 To 200” → includes deposit.
    • Change MWST to “From 100 To 100” → only material net price is taxed.
  • Alternative approach is create a separate subtotal (e.g., “Net price before deposit”) and point MWST to that subtotal. This way, tax is always calculated only on the material net price, regardless of other conditions.

Also tried to set aggregation type to 'None' in the measure settings, but could not see 'None' option available.

entry.title: New ESAC (CQC) Pilot for Customers: Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Community!

We are excited to announce a new Continuous Quality Check (CQC) Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain, available as a pilot offering through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) beginning in Q1 2026.

This new CQC engagement supports customers in validating the technical foundation required to integrate SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX into a unified, interoperable toolchain. The goal is simple: help ensure your environment is ready for seamless end‑to‑end process transformation support.

The Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain focuses on verifying that your technical setup supports:

  • Consistent data flow across SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX
  • Properly established foundational configurations
  • A unified and efficient experience aligned with SAP best practices
  • Safe and confident progression toward go‑live

This CQC provides a remote assessment of key configuration elements needed to integrate these solutions and ensures your setup is prepared for seamless interoperability across the toolchain.

During the CQC engagement, SAP experts will:

  • Review the core configuration prerequisites across SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX
  • Validate whether your setup supports a consistent and efficient toolchain
  • Outline deployment‑readiness observations and identify any integration risks
  • Provide best‑practice recommendations to strengthen your configuration
  • Summarize findings and deliver an action plan in a final report

This engagement helps safeguard your project, ensuring your technical landscape is ready before transformative activities begin.

To participate in this pilot CQC, customers should have:

  • SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and SAP Cloud ALM solutions licensed
  • Administrator permissions in the relevant systems
  • A technical contact available during the engagement
  • An environment already provisioned for initial technical checks

These prerequisites ensure a smooth review and allow the SAP team to provide actionable insights based on your live configuration.

As part of the engagement, you will receive:

  • A remote analysis performed by SAP experts
  • A detailed report outlining the findings
  • Clear recommendations and action items for your configuration

entry.title: Is SAP still looking for its identity?
entry.content:
I've been around SAP since 2004. And lately, I think I begun to understand what's SAP's problem. And I guess the problem is that SAP has not found its identity (yet?).

If SAP was a person, we'd say SAP has ADHD: it's unable to keep focus and changes direction and even behaviour every other day.

SAP is unable to stick around a single concept, architecture, product, even image except the SAP blue logo.

I dunno if it's because its parents are away, because it's grown too much and expanded too far, or if it's because it has too many people trying to justify their salaries.

As it's not my money, I really don't care, except because it made me lose any trust I could have on SAP ever.

Every new SAP announcement flows through my brain like sugar: a small activity peak and then a sense of emptyness.

Maybe it's just me. But I still wonder if some day SAP will find its identity and will be able to stick with it, making my professional life a bit easier.

entry.title: Re: there is no linkage between sales order and STO PO nu...
entry.content:
Good day @lithika_nandhakumar 

Thank you for your question.

In public cloud, yes, there is no single standard CDS view (released) that gives you the process flow you described in one dataset. The public cloud only offers these CDS content specifically for objects e.g., SO, PR, PO etc. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8573b810511948c8a99c0672abc159aa.html for more details.

You can trace the chain E2E through MRP element and pegging views in the Material Coverage apps.

I suggest you:

  • Use Material Coverage and export because it is a standard solution in public cloud if your requirement is to trace and report without building custom CDS joins.

Here is a quick guide:

  • In your tenant, open Monitor Material Coverage – Net and Individual Segments. Filter by your material, plant and segment type. 

  • Open the material and check coverage. Depending on your setup, you will see requirement elements and matching receipts elements e.g., Planned Order, PR and STO. Then you can export and use the data to build your demand tracking report outside the app.

Consult these references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/1ab27d576b8aab76e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/7859921c79da489daf568710762da978.html

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Discover the right SAP solutions — fast.
entry.content:
SAP Solution Scout helps you cut through complexity and find SAP products that match your goals. Instead of digging through documentation, you get a focused, easy-to-navigate view of the SAP portfolio with clear guidance on capabilities and benefits.

What it does

  • Curates solutions for your industry (retail, life sciences, industrial manufacturing, etc.) so you see only relevant options including industry specifics
  • Highlights technical specs and business impact to help you compare choices quickly
  • Uses a modern, user-friendly interface that speeds decision-making

What’s new

  • Powered by the SAP Reference Architecture: Matches are anchored in a consistent business and solution architecture, giving customers, sellers and partners a common language to assess fit and build a clear joint value story.

Why it matters

  • Save time by avoiding irrelevant options
  • Choose solutions that meet your future needs
  • Gain industry-specific insights to stay competitive

Start exploring with SAP Solution Scout and make smarter, faster decisions that drive efficiency and growth.

entry.title: Re: What is standard workflow for Documented Credit Decision
entry.content:

entry.title: Situation Handling for Maintenance Order – Trigger Notification at Post-Execution Phase in SAP Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am working with Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Asset Management / EAM) and have a requirement to notify Production users when a maintenance order reaches the post-execution stage (i.e., maintenance work is completed and production can resume).

I explored the standard situation type
EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED (Maintenance Order Phase Changed).

However, while configuring the conditions / anchor object filters, I noticed that:

  • Order Phase (Execution / Post-Execution) is not available as a condition

  • System Status (CNF / TECO) is also not available as a filter

  • Only header attributes such as Plant, Order Type, Equipment, Dates, etc. are exposed

In contrast, for Maintenance Notifications, phase and subphase are available as conditions, making it easier to trigger notifications.

My questions are:

  1. How can we trigger a situation notification specifically when a maintenance order enters the post-execution stage in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

  2. Is there any standard workaround or recommended approach to achieve this using Situation Handling?

  3. Is this a known limitation by design for maintenance orders in Public Cloud?

The goal is purely to inform Production users (via Fiori notification or email) that the maintenance activity is completed and they can resume operations — without using custom developments, as this is a Public Cloud system.

Any guidance, best practices, or confirmation from SAP experts would be greatly appreciated.


Regards,
Sriram R

entry.title: Re: Hybrid Integration Pattern- SAP S/4HANA Vendor Data to Azure Service Bus Using SAP Integration S
entry.content:
hi @NaveenReddy_06 

This is a very interesting and well-thought-out architecture.
One possible alternative to consider is a more textbook event-driven approach using native SAP capabilities:

S/4HANA Cloud emits a Business Partner event → SAP Event Mesh → Consumer: Cloud Integration (here with API enrichment) → Azure API Management (proxy) → Coupa REST API

With this approach, the source system is decoupled from consumers at the very first step by using near real-time, native event publishing via SAP Event Mesh.
Cloud Integration then focuses only on consumer-specific responsibilities, such as enrichment and transformation required by the target system (Coupa).

This makes possible to onboard additional consumers in the future without impacting existing Iflow, which is coupled to Coupa specific requirements.

br

Mateusz

entry.title: SAP EWM Product Master Upload Best Practices
entry.content:
SAP EWM Product Master Upload Best Practices

Introduction

Product master data forms the foundation of all warehouse operations in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). Proper management of this critical master data directly impacts warehouse efficiency, inventory accuracy, and overall operational performance. SAP Note 3302269 addresses common challenges organizations face when uploading product master data, particularly around required fields in CSV templates and handling empty fields during the upload process.

Understanding and implementing best practices for product master uploads is essential for successful EWM implementations, whether you're migrating from legacy systems, performing regular maintenance, or managing warehouse go-lives. Poor data quality or incorrect upload procedures can lead to operational disruptions, process failures, and significant business impact.

Understanding EWM Product Master Data

EWM product master data consists of multiple layers of information that control how materials behave within warehouse operations. The key components include:

Global Product Data: Basic material information transferred from ERP systems (MM01/MM02/MM03 transactions) stored in tables like MARA, MARC, and MARD. This includes material numbers, descriptions, base units of measure, and general material properties .

Warehouse-Specific Data: EWM-specific attributes stored in table /SAPAPO/MATLWH with key fields PRODUCT, LGNUM (warehouse number), and ENTITLED (party entitled to dispose). This includes put-away control indicators, stock removal indicators, and warehouse-specific processing rules ,.

Storage Type Dependent Data: Detailed storage specifications stored in /SAPAPO/MATLWHST, including storage type assignments, fixed bin indicators, and storage-specific parameters ,.

The relationship between ECC/S4HANA and EWM is hierarchical - basic product views must exist in the ERP system before warehouse-specific extensions can be created in EWM ,.

Pre-Upload Preparation

Data Quality Assessment

Before initiating any upload process, conduct thorough data validation:

  • Mandatory Field Verification: Ensure all required fields are populated, particularly LGNUM (warehouse number) and ENTITLED (party entitled to dispose) for warehouse data 
  • LPTYP Field Validation: When the LPTYP (storage type) field is empty in templates, related fields like maximum and minimum quantities may be cleared during upload
  • Business Rule Consistency: Validate that put-away control indicators, stock removal indicators, and other business rules align with warehouse operational requirements 

System Prerequisites

Verify system readiness before upload execution:

  • ERP Product Existence: Confirm that basic product master data exists in MM01/MM02/MM03 transactions in the source ERP system 
  • Integration Configuration: Validate that all referenced master data (storage types, packaging materials, transportation groups) exists in both ERP and EWM systems 
  • Authorization Setup: Ensure users have appropriate authorizations for product master maintenance and mass upload transactions 

Best Practice Upload Methods

Method 1: Warehouse Monitor Mass Creation

The EWM Warehouse Monitor provides an intuitive interface for mass product master creation and maintenance :

Step-by-Step Process:

  1. Navigate to Warehouse Monitor (/SCWM/MON) → Product Master Data → Warehouse Attributes
  2. Select "Only those products without Warehouse Data" to identify products requiring extension
  3. Select target products and choose "Mass Change/Create" from "More Methods" option
  4. Enter warehouse-specific data in the popup dialog
  5. Confirm the mass update operation

Advantages: User-friendly interface, real-time validation, immediate feedback on data quality issues Limitations: Best suited for smaller datasets, requires manual data entry Recommended Scenarios: Initial warehouse setup, ad-hoc corrections, small-scale maintenance activities 

Method 2: /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT Transaction

This transaction enables batch processing using external CSV files :

Implementation Details:

  1. Download the SAP-provided template "SCWM MIGR fields.xlsx" from SAP Note 2602348
  2. Populate the template with product master data and save as CSV format
  3. Execute transaction /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT
  4. Select "Upload to EWM" and "Product Master Data" options
  5. Specify warehouse number and party entitled to dispose
  6. Upload the CSV file with proper field separator (comma) configuration

Performance Considerations: Handles large datasets efficiently, supports automated processing Use Case Recommendations: Mass migrations, regular bulk updates, integration with external systems ,

Method 3: Fiori Mass Maintenance Application

For S/4HANA 2023 and later versions, the Fiori-based mass maintenance application provides modern user experience ,:

Key Features:

  • Guided process flow through Start → Scope → Selection → Edit → Validation → Activation steps
  • Built-in validation and error handling
  • Export capabilities for verification
  • Integration with Master Data Governance (MDG) framework

Business Role Requirements: SAP_BR_PRODMASTER_SPECIALIST or SAP_MD_MDC_ADMIN_APP_06 

Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them

  1. Empty LPTYP Field Issues
    • Description: When storage type (LPTYP) is empty, related quantity fields get cleared during upload 
    • Prevention Strategy: Always validate storage type assignments before uploading, separate warehouse data from storage type dependent data uploads
  2. Field Separator Configuration
    • Description: Incorrect field separator settings cause CSV parsing failures 
    • Prevention Strategy: Always set field separator to comma (,) in /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT transaction
  3. Missing Prerequisites
    • Description: Attempting to upload warehouse data before basic ERP product exists 
    • Prevention Strategy: Verify ERP product master existence using /SAPAPO/MAT1 before warehouse extension

Performance Optimization Tips

  • Batch Size Management: Process uploads in manageable chunks (typically 1000-5000 records) to avoid system timeouts 
  • Timing Considerations: Schedule large uploads during off-peak hours to minimize system impact
  • Resource Monitoring: Monitor system resources during upload processes, particularly memory and database connections

Validation and Testing

Pre-Production Testing

Establish comprehensive testing procedures:

  • Test Data Preparation: Create representative test datasets covering all product types and scenarios
  • Validation Scenarios: Test both successful uploads and error conditions
  • Performance Benchmarking: Measure upload times and system resource consumption

Post-Upload Verification

Implement systematic verification processes:

  • Data Integrity Checks: Verify data accuracy using warehouse monitor and transaction /SCWM/MAT1 ,
  • Process Flow Validation: Test end-to-end warehouse processes with uploaded products
  • Error Log Analysis: Review upload logs for warnings or partial failures

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Issue

Symptoms

Resolution

CSV Parsing Errors

Upload fails with format errors

Verify field separator settings and CSV structure 

Missing Storage Type Data

Quantity fields cleared after upload

Separate warehouse and storage type uploads, validate LPTYP field 

Authorization Failures

Access denied during upload

Verify user roles and product master authorizations 

Integration Errors

Referenced master data not found

Ensure all dependent master data exists in both systems 

Monitoring and Maintenance

Establish ongoing governance processes:

  • Regular Data Quality Audits: Implement periodic checks for data consistency and completeness
  • Change Management: Define procedures for handling product master changes and updates
  • Performance Monitoring: Track upload performance metrics and optimize as needed

Conclusion

Successful EWM product master uploads require careful planning, proper tool selection, and adherence to established best practices. The choice between warehouse monitor, /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT, or Fiori applications depends on your specific requirements, data volumes, and system landscape.

Key success factors include thorough pre-upload preparation, understanding the hierarchical relationship between ERP and EWM data, and implementing robust validation procedures. By following these best practices and avoiding common pitfalls, organizations can ensure reliable, efficient product master data management that supports optimal warehouse operations.

Additional Resources

  • SAP Note 3302269: EWM: Best Practices to Upload Product Master ,
  • SAP Note 2602348: Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4HANA embedded EWM ,
  • SAP Note 2627825: Mass Creation of Warehouse Products from Monitor,

 Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: Where-used list of method with code line
entry.content: I like to use program RS_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN. You put in a string, which can be your method name, and limit by Z* packages or more specific if you want. The program will return all places where it found that string, displaying the actual code line and a few lines above and below it. You can also double click and it will take you to the code object in display mode.

entry.title: Re: SAP Change document integration on RAP - I_ChangeDocu...
entry.content:
The SAP documentation is misleading here. CDS views like I_ChangeDocument_2 are referenced in the docs but are not released and cannot be used in the BTP ABAP Environment.

The working solution to handle everything on the backend:

  • Enable change documents in the RAP behavior definition.

  • Create a custom CDS Custom Entity implemented via a Query Provider.

  • Read change documents using the class CL_CHDO_READ_TOOLS 

TRY.
    cl_chdo_read_tools=>changedocument_read(
      EXPORTING
        i_objectclass = 'ZCUSTOMOBJECT'
        it_objectid   = lt_objid
      IMPORTING
        et_cdredadd_tab = lt_redadd
      CHANGING
        ct_cdhdr        = lt_cdhdr
    ).
  CATCH cx_chdo_read_error.
    RETURN.
ENDTRY.

The main root then has an association to this custom CDS entity, and the Change Log is displayed in the Object Page.


So the solution is custom CDS + query provider + released API, fully backend-driven.

entry.title: Partner‑Driven Innovation Takes Off with Agent Builder in Joule Studio
entry.content:
In 2025, SAP expanded the Joule ecosystem with Joule Studio, enabling partners to extend Joule through custom skills and autonomous agents. Earlier initiatives demonstrated how partners could build Joule skills to handle targeted workflows and augment enterprise processes, as showcased in the Hack2Build custom Joule skills edition . 

With the Agent Builder now generally available, (read the announcement blog here), SAP has taken a major step forward. Partners can use agentic AI to design custom AI agents capable of planning, reasoning, and executing multi‑step processes across SAP and non‑SAP systems, supported by capabilities such as MCP‑based integrations, multi‑agent orchestration, and business‑context grounding. 

Against this backdrop, the Hack2Build on Custom AI Agents invited partners to explore these capabilities hands‑on for this inaugural edition. 

 

Hack2Build Custom AI Agents Edition 

The Hack2Build Custom AI Agents Edition focused on a select group of early‑adopter partners who received hands‑on coaching from SAP experts to work with the newly released Agent Builder capability. SAP Product and solution architecture specialists supported partners in shaping their designs and aligning them with specific customer needs.

SAP Partners participated in Hack2Build custom AI Agents first edition- January 2026

 Partners overcame early product‑maturity challenges and succeeded in building functional custom AI agents in less than seven days during the hack phase. The initiative culminated in a Live Demo Jam, where all teams presented their working prototypes to a panel of SAP judges.

This was the first Hack2Build where partners worked directly with agentic AI tooling, experimenting with autonomous reasoning, multi‑agent orchestration, system integration patterns, and MCP‑enabled workflows.

🏆Winners

The winning solutions stood out for addressing customer‑critical challenges while thoughtfully complementing SAP’s portfolio. With creative use of agentic AI, the teams demonstrated how autonomous agents can be embedded into real workflows—connecting systems, reasoning over business data, and driving outcomes that matter to customers.

🥇Winner — LTIMindtree: Treasury Auto Ops

Treasury Auto Ops applies agentic AI to transform treasury management with real‑time forecasting, FX exposure assessment, liquidity oversight, and automated hedging recommendations. The agent synthesizes market indicators, macroeconomic signals, and internal treasury data to reach high forecasting accuracy and proactively execute SAP Treasury actions with policy‑compliant governance.
Partners selected for Hack2Build custom AI Agents first edition- January 2026

🥈1st Runner‑Up — Avvale: Early Payment Assistant

Avvale's agent identifies early‑payment discount opportunities across supplier invoices and provides optimized payment recommendations. By balancing liquidity needs with discount potential, it improves working capital, strengthens supplier relationships, and delivers measurable ROI.
Avvale: Early Payment Assistant

 🥉 2nd Runner‑Up — Talan: TALK2BUY

TALK2BUY brings conversational procurement to SAP Cloud ERP. Users can speak or type purchase requisitions in natural language, while the agent enforces procurement policy, recommends compliant items, and sends Microsoft Teams reminders to ensure timely goods‑receipt updates.

Talan: TALK2BUY

 ⭐ Finalists: High‑Quality Solutions from All Participating Partners

  • All for One – Service preparation, escalation & ticket automation agent
  • Capgemini – Smart Vendor Agent to assess supplier data consistency 
  • cbs  – ARIA: Agentic Rate & Inforecord Automation agent
  • DXC Technology – AI Engineering Proposal Assistant (JAEPA)
  • KPMG – Inventory Reconciliation Agent
  • Neuman – Financial Performance Advisor
  • NTT Data – FraudCheck Agent
  • Sodales – Job Accommodation & Return‑to‑Work program generator

Special Thanks

A sincere thank you to all participating partners, judges Pavel Penaz, Jan Schneider, Stefan Koenig, Boris Andree, the SAP Build and Joule Studio product teams, and the wider SAP Partner Ecosystem Success units for their support throughout the hack phase.

What’s Next?

The Hack2Build initiative now moves into the BUILD phase, where all participating partners will work over the next 4–5 weeks to realize the full scope of their agentic solutions. Coached by SAP experts, this phase focuses on maturing designs, enriching integrations, and preparing customer‑ready outcomes based on real business requirements and partner domain expertise.

For partners  interested in joining future Hack2Build, registration for the next edition in March is already open.

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry API: HSN/SAC Code Maintenance
entry.content:
Good day @gaurav7 

Thank you for your question.

First let’s address a key point.

With Journal Entry-Post (Asynchronous) you cannot post tax relevant journal entries. HSN or SAC is not an input it supports as a dedicated field in this case for India GST. See Maintain HSN/SAC for more insights.

In you snippet, you used ProductTaxItem to carry tax related item information for the journal entry posting. This is not the standard mechanism to feed HSN or SAC into the Indian accounting document. KBA 3349584 and Guidelines for API Journal Entry – Post provide good details on how to go about it.

So, my suggestion is:

  • Not to try to pass HSN or SAC through ProductiveTaxText for the journal entry post API. The reason is, it is not the standard HSN or SAC integration point.
  • Maintain HSN/SAC in your configuration and assign it in product or service master.
  • If you really need to have HSN or SAC reflected, then use a business document posting process e.g., supplier invoice or billing where the system can derive it from the item context.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Missing Legally Required Company Address Fields for E...
entry.content:
Hello Chris,

Thank you for your detailed response and for sharing the recommended patterns. However, we still see a gap and would appreciate some clarification, as it is not clear how the full company address can actually be retrieved in practice using only released standard APIs in S/4HANA Public Cloud. Specifically:

• While API_COMPANYCODE_SRV does indeed expose an AddressID, there is currently no released A2X API in Public Cloud that allows us to resolve this AddressID into full address details (street, house number, postal code, city, country, etc.).

• Accessing Business Address Services to read the address behind the AddressID does not appear to be possible via a released standard API. From our understanding, consuming BAS directly would require the development of a custom API or non released artifacts, which is not permitted in Public Cloud, at least not in our standard approach.

• The Location API (API_LOCATION_SRV) would technically solve this, but it is not available in our tenant and cannot be activated at this stage due to scope limitations.

Regarding the Business Partner approach: We do have Business Partners that correspond to our Company Codes. However, the relationship between Company Code and BP is not exposed in a standard, consumable way via released APIs. As a result, using API_BUSINESS_PARTNER would require us to maintain a custom Company Code ↔ Business Partner mapping table on the integration side. This introduces:

• additional custom logic and long term maintenance,

• a dependency that is not enforced or governed by the standard S/4HANA Public Cloud data model,

• and a deviation from our standard architecture principles, which aim to rely exclusively on native relationships and released APIs, without custom mapping for fundamental legal data such as the seller’s registered address.

So, while the BP-based approach is theoretically possible, it is not fully standard nor sustainable for our scenario without introducing custom artifacts.

Could you please clarify:

• Which released A2X API is intended to be used to read Business Address Services by AddressID in Public Cloud?

• Or is the expectation that customers must either:

- activate Location Management scope items, or

- rely on Business Partners with customer specific mapping logic? This clarification would be very helpful, as the missing seller address is currently a blocking issue for Belgian Peppol e invoicing compliance.

Thank you again for your support and insights.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: How to display total values on top of each bar in a 1...
entry.content:
@benhaddou 

In stacked column chart the totals are only shown when the chart is set to display values. The moment it's set to show as 100%, the chart only shows the split %. There is no standard way to show the absolute totals on a stacked chart set to display as 100%. The only work around could be using a separate chart to  display as absolutes and layer them over each other but this would also be very difficult.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Kyma High availability
entry.content: In most of the cases, the nodes themselves are updated one by one. The maintenance windows schedules as per region are mentioned in the link shared previously. https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/systems-installations/cac/maintenance-windows.html?anchorId=section_1497935568

entry.title: Re: In SAC, how to setup aggregation type 'None' for meas...
entry.content:
@Krishnaprakash_ 

You should check couple of things. Check what is the structure priority of your model. You can check it in model preferences under structure priority. Set it to be driven from accounts if its set to Measures. Also if you want the measures to aggregate as none, you would have to set the exception aggregation as None with all dimensions selected for aggregation. Measures do not allow standard aggregation as None.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: [BDC-SAC] Akamai CDN , Metadata Understanding Data Download When Access SAC
entry.content:
Understanding Data Download Behavior When Accessing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

When accessing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) through a web browser, several external URLs are involved.
At a high level, these can be categorized into static content delivered via CDN and dynamic, tenant-specific metadata.

Static Content: Assets and SAPUI5 via Akamai CDN

By default, SAC loads static resources such as:

  • Assets

  • SAPUI5 libraries

These resources are delivered through the Akamai Content Delivery Network (CDN).
Because they are static and globally distributed, they are highly optimized for performance and reuse.

Dynamic Content: Tenant-Specific Metadata

In addition to static content, SAC also retrieves metadata associated with the customer tenant, such as:

  • User- and role-related metadata

  • Model and story metadata

  • Session-related configuration data

These requests are typically served directly from SAP data centers rather than the CDN.

A Common Korean Customer Question: “How Much Data Is Downloaded Every Time?”

A frequent question arises around the relative data volume downloaded from:

  • Akamai CDN (static content) versus

  • SAP data centers (dynamic metadata)

This question often includes the phrase “data downloaded every time”, which can be interpreted in multiple ways and is sometimes misunderstood.

Why “Downloaded Every Time” Is Misleading

In practice, data is not downloaded on every access in the literal sense:

  • Browser caching plays a critical role

  • Cached data may reside in memory cache or disk cache

  • Even when cached, loading data from cache still incurs some processing time, but it does not involve network download

As a result:

  • Static content served via Akamai CDN is rarely re-downloaded

  • Dynamic metadata is also not fully re-downloaded on every request, but fetched incrementally and conditionally based on session state and cache validation

What HAR File Analysis Typically Shows

When analyzing real-world HAR (HTTP Archive) files, a consistent pattern emerges:

  • In terms of request volume and payload size,
    static content delivered via Akamai CDN accounts for the majority of data transferred

  • Dynamic metadata requests are generally smaller in size and more selective

This means that, from a network and performance perspective, Akamai-delivered static resources dominate the overall traffic profile, especially during initial access or first-time load scenarios.

Architectural Implication: Regional Tenants and Legal Entities

This observation has an important architectural implication.

For customers operating multiple legal entities across regions, there is often concern that:

“Separating tenants by region may negatively impact network traffic or performance.”



However, given that:

  • The majority of transferred data is static and CDN-delivered

  • Static content is shared and globally optimized

  • Tenant-specific metadata represents a smaller portion of total traffic

There is no inherent disadvantage—from a data transfer or performance standpoint—in operating multiple regional tenants, when required for legal, regulatory, or organizational reasons.


Understanding how SAC content is delivered—and how browser caching actually works—helps clarify common misconceptions around “repeated downloads.”

In reality:

  • Most data is not repeatedly downloaded

  • Akamai CDN plays a dominant role in overall data transfer

  • Tenant separation decisions should be driven by governance and compliance, not by unfounded concerns about network inefficiency

This technical insight provides a solid, data-backed foundation for making informed architectural decisions in SAP Analytics Cloud deployments.

entry.title: Accelerating SAP Digital Manufacturing with SAP Test Automation by Tricentis in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In today’s fast‑moving manufacturing landscape, standing still is not an option. With quarterly SAP Digital Manufacturing releases, evolving shop‑floor processes, and constant pressure to scale, maintaining a stable and reliable system becomes a challenge. Every rollout, upgrade, or plant onboarding demands repeated testing and when done manually, this quickly turns into a time‑consuming bottleneck. 

The good news? SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis allows you to accelerate delivery, tighten quality, and free your team from repetitive validation work, and many SAP customers can get started using tools they already own. Whether you’re operating one plant or fifty, automation helps ensure your SAP Digital Manufacturing processes run smoothly, release after release.

 

Why automate testing in SAP Digital Manufacturing?

Across SAP Digital Manufacturing implementations, similar challenges appear:
manual regression cycles are time‑intensive, multiple process variants must be validated repeatedly, and quarterly SAP releases require consistent checks to ensure integrations and shop floor processes behave as expected.

Automation tackles these pain points by:

  • Reducing repetitive manual testing, especially across multiple plants
  • Shortening regression cycles, enabling faster rollout of new features
  • Increasing stability and coverage, even as processes evolve
  • Freeing business users for meaningful testing, not repetitive validation

Instead of re-running known scenarios every quarter, teams can focus their time on what’s new or business‑critical.

🧰 What’s in your toolkit? (You already own it!)

If you have SAP Enterprise Support, cloud edition(s), you likely already have access to:

SAP Cloud ALM

Your central hub for test preparation, planning, orchestration, and defect management.
✔ Included in your subscription
✔ Up to 24 GB data

For more information, please refer to usage rights for SAP Cloud ALM.

SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis

Included entitlement with:

✔ 5 named users

✔ 5 execution agents

✔ 500 automated test runs/month

✔ 2.5 GB storage

To learn more, please refer to usage rights for SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis

The setup is done very easily. Tricentis integration in SAP Cloud ALM can be ready in just minutes. Click here to learn more.

 

From manual to automated: How it works

1️⃣ Document scenarios in SAP Cloud ALM

  • Capture end‑to‑end flows, from order creation all the way to shop‑floor execution.

2️⃣ Mark tests as automated

  • SAP Cloud ALM directly links your test cases to Tricentis for execution.

3️⃣Scan and build automation in Tricentis

  • Capture Operator PODs and SAP DM UI elements
  • Build automation flows with drag‑and‑drop modules
  • Parameterize your data for reuse across plants, variants, and releases

4️⃣ Execute & analyze

  • Run your automated tests from Tricentis or SAP Cloud ALM
  • Review logs, screenshots, and step‑by‑step recordings
  • Track defects and trigger re‑runs directly in SAP Cloud ALM

 

Best practices

  • Begin with stable, repetitive processes

  • Combine UI automation with API validations
  • Use parameterized data — avoid hard-coded materials/SFCs
  • Keep automation updated with each release
  • Assign clear ownership for automation assets

 

Common pitfalls when starting test automation

Implementing SAP Test Automation in SAP Digital Manufacturing is not only a technical task. It requires the right mix of business process knowledge, test methodology expertise, and automation skills. A small, dedicated expert team is essential to set the foundation correctly from the start, and many organizations benefit from engaging specialized partners from SAP’s growing test automation ecosystem.

There are several pitfalls that frequently occur when customers begin their automation journey — from organizational setup to preparation and script implementation. A few key ones include:

🎯 Starting automation without a clear test strategy or SAP DM–specific objectives
Ensure clarity on what you want to achieve, how testing will be organized, and which processes automation must protect.

👥 Assigning automation solely to business users
Testing requires dedicated ownership; business experts contribute scenarios, but automation should be owned and maintained by a skilled team.

🌱 Trying to automate everything at once
Start small, focus on stable and high‑value scenarios, and grow automation iteratively.

🧩 Using static or hard‑coded data
This limits flexibility across plants, variants, and releases, and reduces confidence in test results.

These represent only a subset of common challenges. We will continue expanding this guidance, and customers are encouraged to collaborate with partners who bring proven methodology and automation experience.

 

Further resources

  • A beginner session on ensuring quality at speed with SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis testing solutions
  • Webcast recording: Cloud ALM for Implementation of SAP Digital Manufacturing (2024)
  • Try out our SAP Cloud ALM public demo tenant 
  • Learn more about SAP Cloud ALM 

 

What's next?

To help you build a strong SAP Test Automation foundation in SAP Digital Manufacturing and make the most of it, we will be launching a dedicated webcast series soon. Stay tuned, more details are coming soon! 🚀

entry.title: [BDC-SAC] IP Whitelist and Access Control in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) does not provide a native, built-in feature for IP-based whitelist or IP access control at application level. However, IP control can be effectively implemented through Identity Provider (IdP) integration.

IP Control via IdP and SSO Integration

In most customer environments, SAC is integrated with the customer’s existing SSO infrastructure by connecting to an external IdP.
When SAC is accessed through such an SSO setup:

  • IP-based access restrictions can be enforced at the IdP or SSO system level
  • This allows customers to control which IP ranges or network zones are permitted to authenticate and access SAC
  • As a result, while SAC itself does not perform IP filtering, IP access control is commonly achieved through the customer’s identity and authentication layer, which aligns well with enterprise security architectures.

IP Range Characteristics

  • IP ranges used by SAP data centers are fixed (End point)
  • The specific IP range depends on the data center region where the SAC tenant is hosted
  • Customers can identify and validate the relevant IP ranges based on the tenant’s regional deployment

SAP provides an official and up-to-date reference for these IP ranges and endpoints, which can be found here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/regions-and-api-endpoints-available-for-cloud-foundry-environment

entry.title: Von Werkzeugen zu Wegweisern - MHPs Erfolgsmodell in der SAP Berater Ausbildung
entry.content:
Die SAP Training und Change Management Community Session am 22.01.2026 beleuchtete die Herausforderungen und Lösungsansätze bei der Ausbildung von SAP-Beratern und -ExpertInnen. Nicole Heymann, Thomas Baier und Friederike Kruse von MHP teilten ihre Erfahrungen zur strukturierten Kompetenzentwicklung im SAP-Umfeld.

Die MHP-Lernlandschaft: Blended Learning als Grundprinzip

MHP setzt auf einen Blended Learning Ansatz, der unterschiedliche Lerntypen und Bedarfe berücksichtigt. Die Bandbreite reicht von organisierten Formaten wie Classroom-Trainings und Remote-Schulungen über Events wie die Failure Heroes Night bis hin zu selbstgesteuerten Lernangeboten.

Zentral ist die Nutzung des SAP Learning Hub für die SAP Weiterbildung. Ergänzend kommen Communities, Network-Events, Feedback-Runden und Mentoring-Programme zum Einsatz. Der Ansatz verbindet Hard Skills mit Human Skills, um eine ganzheitliche Entwicklung zu ermöglichen.

Learning Journeys: Rollenspezifische Entwicklungspfade

Learning Journeys sind bei MHP rollenspezifische Lernpfade, die sich an verschiedenen Mitarbeiterrollen orientieren.

Die Journeys dauern im Durchschnitt drei Jahre und kombinieren verschiedene Formate: Onsite-Trainings, Web-based Trainings, Online-Kurse von Plattformen wie LinkedIn Learning sowie Community-Austausch. Der Mix wird je nach Zielgruppengröße und Lernbedarf angepasst.

Level der Befähigung: Vom Beginner zum Expert Level

Die SAP-Ausbildung bei MHP gliedert sich in drei Stufen. Beginner erhalten zunächst umfassendes Basiswissen zu SAP-Prozessen, Tools wie dem Cloud Application Lifecycle Management, der SAP Activate-Methodik, Signavio oder der Business Technology Platform. Sie werden im Projekt durch Paten oder zertifizierte Consultants begleitet.

Im zweiten Schritt erfolgt die Spezialisierung auf ein SAP-Portfolio wie Vendor Management oder Supply Chain. Die Learning Journey endet mit einer offiziellen SAP-Zertifizierung.

Für den Expert Level werden zusätzliche Zertifizierungen in angrenzenden Fachgebieten empfohlen. Experten tauschen dabei ihre Erfahrungen in Expertengruppen aus. Sie treffen sich dabei in Gruppen zu Projektarchitektur, Technologiearchitektur oder Prozessarchitektur und betrachten themenübergreifend Gesamtprozesse und Gesamtarchitekturen.

Die zentrale SAP Wissensplattform bei MHP

Die interne Website bündelt seit sechs Jahren alle SAP-relevanten Tools und Ressourcen an einem Ort. Sie umfasst eigene Templates für Projekte, ein Prozesshaus angelehnt an SAP-Standards, eine umfangreiche Demo Landscape mit integrierten SAP-Modulen sowie alle Learning Journeys.

 

Die Plattform ist in 16 Kategorien gegliedert, die sich am Portfolio und am SAP Competency Framework orientieren. Aktuell gibt es 65 Learning Journeys, die regelmäßig aktualisiert und um Experten-Input ergänzt werden. Damit die Inhalte aktuell bleiben sind teilweise SAP Inhalte auch direkt eingebunden.

SAP Unlocked: Das Basis-Training für SAP-Exoten

Das Training "SAP Unlocked" richtet sich an SAP-Neulinge und Quereinsteiger aus nicht-technischen Bereichen. Es verfolgt drei zentrale Ziele:

  • Einheitliches Grundverständnis schaffen, damit alle die gleiche Sprache sprechen
  • Grundlage für fundierte Entscheidungen und bessere Zusammenarbeit legen
  • Eigene Themen in den Gesamtkontext einbetten können

Das circa vierstündige Training kann virtuell oder vor Ort durchgeführt werden. Es richtet sich an 20 bis 25 Teilnehmende, um optimalen Austausch zu gewährleisten. Der Fokus liegt auf Praxisnähe und Verständlichkeit.

Trainingsinhalte: Von SAP-Grundlagen bis zur Systemdemo

Das Training beginnt mit einer SAP-Einführung zu Modulen und dem ERP-System. Es folgen die SAP-Projektphasen und die Methodik SAP Activate mit Fokus auf cross-funktionale Streams wie Change Management und Training. Auch Hybrid-agile Projektmethodik und Project Excellence werden behandelt.

Weitere Module umfassen Prozesse, Streams und das MHP SAP Process House. Eine Systemdemo vermittelt visuelle Eindrücke und erzeugt Aha-Effekte. Quiz-Elemente sorgen für Interaktivität und wirken der Vergessenskurve entgegen.

Ein besonderes Element ist das SAP-Vokabelheft im PDF-Format. Es enthält SAP-spezifische und projektspezifische Begriffe mit weiterführenden Links zum SAP Learning Space, zum MHP SAP Learning Space und zu YouTube-Videos. Es dient zudem als Spickzettel für Meetings und Projekteinsätze.

Kundenprojekte: Enablement über die eigene Organisation hinaus

Das SAP Unlocked Training wird auch bei Kunden eingesetzt, um Projektteams und Business-Seite abzuholen. Es wird dann kundenspezifisch angepasst, inklusive individualisiertem Vokabelheft. Die Erfahrung zeigt: Ein informiertes Team reduziert Widerstände und verbessert die Zusammenarbeit.

Technologie und Tools: SAP Success Factors und SharePoint

Die Lerninfrastruktur basiert auf SAP Success Factors Learning für Trainingsadministration und SAP Learning Journeys. Die Kommunikation läuft über SharePoint, wo auch die MHP loves SAP Website gehostet wird.

Intern wurde ein KI-Tool aufgesetzt, das auf alle internen SharePoint-Inhalte zugreift. Berater können damit nach Schulungen, Templates und Tools suchen und sich beraten lassen. Das Tool vergleicht MHP-Angebote mit SAP-Angeboten.

Für spezielle Zielgruppen gibt es auch eine interne Lern-App zur Vorbereitung auf Zertifizierungen. Bei größeren Zielgruppen werden Inhalte selbst kuratiert und ergänzt, bei kleineren Gruppen werden SAP-Inhalte direkt übernommen.

KI-Enablement: Eigene Learning Journeys für Developer und Consultants

MHP hat umfangreiche Learning Journeys zum Thema KI entwickelt. Für Developer gibt es eine spezielle Journey mit rechtlichen Aspekten und Best Practices. Eine entsprechende Journey existiert auch für Consultants.

Auf den Lernplattformen wird geprüft, inwieweit KI-basierte Lernpfadempfehlungen die manuell erstellten Learning Journeys ergänzen können. Parallel läuft das KI Learning Enablement für die gesamte MHP-Organisation, um alle Mitarbeitenden im Umgang mit KI zu befähigen.

Aktualität und Qualitätssicherung

Die quartalsweisen SAP-Updates werden in Expertengremien gesichtet und bewertet. Nicht jede Änderung führt zu Anpassungen der Learning Journeys. Relevanzkritisch sind vor allem Änderungen aus dem Competency Framework oder strategischen Anforderungen.

Inhalte werden bewusst verkürzt oder zusammengefasst, um die Quintessenz auf den Punkt zu bringen. Im Expert Level geht es weniger um Zertifizierung als um Leading-Edge-Wissen aus Workshops und Veranstaltungen.

Die Experten-Community sorgt dafür, dass neue Tools und Lösungen in Trainings für Beginner einfließen und der Wissenskreislauf geschlossen bleibt.

Inspirierende Aussagen aus der Session

  1. "Die Liebe zu SAP entdecken" – Es geht darum, Begeisterung für SAP zu wecken, nicht nur technisches Wissen zu vermitteln.
  2. "Es ist kein reines IT-Thema" – SAP verbindet IT, Business und alle Bereiche dazwischen. Dieses Verständnis ist erfolgskritisch.
  3. "Wir schauen alle auf dasselbe und sehen was anderes" – Verschiedene Perspektiven bereichern SAP-Projekte und erfordern ein gemeinsames Grundverständnis.
  4. "Die Beständigkeit und Qualität von SAP hat etwas für sich" – In einer kurzlebigen IT-Welt bietet SAP nachhaltige Qualität und Verlässlichkeit.

Praktische Tipps für SAP-Enablement

  • Niederschwellig starten: Mit Basistrainings beginnen, die SAP-Begrifflichkeiten erklären, damit sich niemand abgehängt fühlt.
  • Visuell unterstützen: Systemdemos einbauen, damit Teilnehmende sehen, wie SAP aussieht und arbeitet.
  • Hands-On Unterstützung: Ein Vokabelheft mit Fachbegriffen und weiterführenden Links hilft im Projektalltag.
  • Verschiedene Formate kombinieren: Blended Learning mit Classroom, Online-Kursen, Communities und Mentoring erreicht unterschiedliche Lerntypen.
  • Experten einbinden: Der Austausch zwischen Beginnern und Experten schließt den Wissenskreislauf und hält Inhalte aktuell.
  • Buddy-System nutzen: Neue Berater sollten im Projekt von Paten oder zertifizierten Consultants begleitet werden.
  • Business abholen: Auch nicht-technische Teams brauchen SAP-Grundverständnis für erfolgreiche Zusammenarbeit.
  • Interaktivität fördern: Quizze und Diskussionen steigern Aufmerksamkeit und Lernerfolg.

Fragen und Antworten aus der Session 

Anbei die Zusammenfassung aller Fragen und Antworten aus dem Chat und der Diskussion. Einige Punkte haben wir oben schon beschrieben, ich führe sie trotzdem im Q&A Format nochmals auf.

Frage: Welche Rolle spielen Gamification und Personas?

Antwort: Gamification wird kontextabhängig eingesetzt – mehr bei Soft Skills, weniger bei SAP-Zertifizierungen. Eine interne Lern-App unterstützt die Prüfungsvorbereitung. Personas werden vor allem bei Learning Journeys und Karrieremodellen genutzt.

 

Frage: Welches Tool wird für Learning Journeys genutzt?

Antwort: SAP Success Factors Learning für Trainingsadministration und Learning Journeys. SharePoint für Kommunikation und die Website "MHP loves SAP". Beides arbeitet im Zusammenspiel.

 

Frage: Wie funktioniert das Enablement und die Aktualisierungen bei quartalsweisen SAP S/4HANA-Updates und verschiedenen Systemlandschaften?

Antwort: Expertengremien sichten Release-Updates und bewerten die Relevanz. Nur strategisch wichtige Änderungen fließen quartalsweise in Learning Journeys ein. Für Spezialthemen wird auf den Learning Hub verwiesen statt eigene Journeys zu bauen.

 

Frage: Ist die Lern-App öffentlich verfügbar? Ist die Website zugänglich?

Antwort: Beide sind MHP-intern. Kuratierte Inhalte können im Nachgang extern geteilt werden.

 

Frage: In welchem Format liegt das Vokabelheft vor?

Antwort: Als PDF mit weiterführenden Links zu Learning Space, MHP-Ressourcen und YouTube-Videos. Bewusst niederschwellig als Spickzettel für Meetings nutzbar – digital oder ausgedruckt.

 

Frage: Wie werden Language Models und generative KI eingesetzt?

Antwort: MHP nutzt ein internes KI-Tool für die Suche in allen SharePoint-Inhalten, Schulungen und Templates. Auf einigen Lernplattformen werden KI-basierte personalisierte Lernpfadempfehlungen getestet. Eigene umfangreiche Learning Journeys zu KI existieren für Developer und Consultants incl. mit rechtlichen Aspekten.

 

Frage: Sind die Inhalte von SAP oder eigene? Welche Zertifizierungen gibt es?

Antwort: Learning Journeys sind MHP-intern, enden aber mit offiziellen SAP-Zertifizierungen. Bei großen Zielgruppen werden eigene Inhouse-Trainings entwickelt, bei kleinen Gruppen SAP-Inhalte übernommen bzw. referenziert. Inhalte werden auch gekürzt, um die Quintessenz zu vermitteln. Im Expertenlevel geht es um Leading-Edge-Wissen aus externen Workshops statt um Zertifizierungen.

 

Frage: Gibt es auch externe SAP Schulungen von MHP?

Antwort: Schulungen werden teilweise im Rahmen von Beratungsaufträgen angeboten und kundenspezifisch angepasst. Sonst dreht sich das Gezeigte primär um die Weiterbildung von MHP Mitarbeitenden.

 

Zusammenfassung

Die Session zeigte, wie strukturiertes SAP-Enablement von der Basis bis zur Expertenebene funktionieren kann. Der Schlüssel liegt in rollenspezifischen Learning Journeys, die technisches Wissen mit anderen Skills verbinden und durch Blended Learning verschiedene Lerntypen ansprechen.

Interessant fand ich das SAP Unlocked Training für Quereinsteiger und nicht-technische Rollen. Es schafft ein gemeinsames Verständnis und ermöglicht es allen Projektbeteiligten, fundierte Entscheidungen zu treffen. Das SAP-Vokabelheft als praktisches Tool unterstützt den Transfer ins Projekt.

Wie man die Liebe zu SAP entdecken kann, war eine der inspirierenden und positiven Diskussionen am Ende. Ein guter Vorsatz – nicht nur für BeraterInnen. 😊

Kommt gerne in unsere nächste nächste Session KI-Kompetenzentwicklung & Weiterbildung
- Infos und Registrierung wie immer auf der Seite der SAP Learning & Change Kaffee-Ecke

 

Weitere Ressourcen

SAP Corporate Glossary 2025 - PDF-Dokument mit SAP-Fachbegriffen
